([Russian](/wiki/Russian_language "Russian language"): Пётр Петров; born 28 March 1983) is a Russian [professional boxer](/wiki/Professional_boxer "Professional boxer"). He has challenged twice for a world title, at [light-welterweight](/wiki/Light-welterweight "Light-welterweight") in 2011 and at [lightweight](/wiki/Lightweight "Lightweight") in 2017.

**Petr Petrov** ([Russian](/wiki/Russian_language "Russian language"): Пётр Петров; born 28 March 1983) is a Russian [professional boxer](/wiki/Professional_boxer "Professional boxer"). He has challenged twice for a world title, at [light-welterweight](/wiki/Light-welterweight "Light-welterweight") in 2011 and at [lightweight](/wiki/Lightweight "Lightweight") in 2017.

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Petr_Petrov&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

At the age of sixteen Petrov moved to [Madrid](/wiki/Madrid "Madrid"), Spain, as his parents worked there on business. He resides and trains there to this day, and speaks fluent Spanish as well as some English.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Professional career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Petr_Petrov&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Professional career")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/98/Boxcino-Fights-Petrov-Popazov_%2812842392225%29.jpg/250px-Boxcino-Fights-Petrov-Popazov_%2812842392225%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Boxcino-Fights-Petrov-Popazov_(12842392225).jpg)

Petrov (right) vs. Papazov, 2014

Petrov made his professional debut on 10 November 2000, winning a four-round [points decision](/wiki/Points_decision "Points decision") against Gabriel Pedro Silva Guerra. Fighting almost exclusively in Spain for the next decade, Petrov received his first world championship opportunity on 23 September 2011, when he faced [WBA (Regular)](/wiki/World_Boxing_Association#Super_titles "World Boxing Association") [super-lightweight](/wiki/Super-lightweight "Super-lightweight") champion [Marcos Maidana](/wiki/Marcos_Maidana "Marcos Maidana"). In front of Maidana's native Argentine crowd, Petrov suffered two [knockdowns](/wiki/Knockout#Knockdown "Knockout") and was [knocked out](/wiki/Knockout "Knockout") in four rounds.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Throughout 2014, Petrov fought in the United States for the first time and participated in [ESPN](/wiki/ESPN "ESPN")'s Boxcino [lightweight](/wiki/Lightweight "Lightweight") tournament, as part of their *[Friday Night Fights](/wiki/Friday_Night_Fights "Friday Night Fights")* series. He won his quarter- and semi-final matches against Fedor Papazov (six-round [unanimous decision](/wiki/Unanimous_decision "Unanimous decision") on 21 February) and Chris Rudd (fourth-round [stoppage](/wiki/Technical_knockout "Technical knockout") on 28 March), and the tournament final on 23 May by stopping Fernando Carcamo in eight rounds.[[3]](#cite_note-3) During this time he also won the regional WBA–[NABA](/wiki/North_American_Boxing_Association "North American Boxing Association") and [WBO](/wiki/World_Boxing_Organization "World Boxing Organization")–[NABO](/wiki/North_American_Boxing_Organization "North American Boxing Organization") titles. On 30 September 2016, Petrov dominated Michael Pérez in bloody fashion to force a [corner stoppage](/wiki/Corner_stoppage "Corner stoppage") in the sixth round, which earned him a mandatory opportunity for both the WBA and WBO lightweight world titles.[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)

On 23 January 2017, promoter [Frank Warren](/wiki/Frank_Warren_(promoter) "Frank Warren (promoter)") confirmed that Petrov would face WBO lightweight champion [Terry Flanagan](/wiki/Terry_Flanagan_(boxer) "Terry Flanagan (boxer)") on 8 April, in [Manchester](/wiki/Manchester "Manchester").[[6]](#cite_note-6)
